entry.title: Re: Story Reports - Classic Design Experience (CDE) - Deprecation Warning
entry.content:
Hello Conny,

To your question about Stories functionality, yes there will be a retirement of the Classic Experience reports in favor of using the Optimized Story Experience.   This is expected sometime in 2025, but we yet to have full details from the SAC team.  More information can be found on their blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/important-notice-changes-in-sap-analytics-cloud-classic-stories-amp/ba-p/13705220

We also have a blog showing how simple it is to convert to OSE, please check out the content there too:

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/stories-in-people-analytics-optimized-story-experience-ose/ba-p/227281 

Thanks!
Ben

entry.title: Requested data was not found - Fiori object page
entry.content:
Hello, I have a list report application, and when I click onto the object page, I sometimes get the error "Requested data was not found":

On the object page, all there is, is a Sales doc, and sold to party information in the header, and all sales documents have that info, so it's not that the data itself doesn't exist.
I have found the different circumstances under which this error page appears:

1. If the data for the header is not on the list report column, then it gives the error, e.g.:

Since the table does not have sold to party, then I get the error on the object page, if I do include the column, then I don't get the error on the next page:

 -> 

The second, and more important set of circumstance when I get the error, is if a column does not have any info, e.g.:

Since subsequent document is empty for this contract (i.e. the contract was not renewed), the error appears. But for my table I will have columns that will sometimes have no info (like Subsequent Document), but I will also need to be able to click on to the object page. Does anyone have a solution for getting this "Requested data not found" error on the object page?

entry.title: Re: Scheduling after Activity Confirmation in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: Hi, if activities/WBS are scheduled in the past or not highly depends on the settings (see the indicator "start in the past" in Kens post).

entry.title: Re: Using OData in a Ui-Integration Card (Component)
entry.content: Hello!

entry.title: Re: Scheduling after Activity Confirmation in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Exactly, that's what I also always recommend to do in such scenarios.

In addition you have to reschedule the WBS/project in order to push the dates bottom-up to the WBS. Else only the activity will keep the actual dates. If you don't want to do that manually, the indicator "automatic scheduling at saving" may fulfill that. But that depends on your overall project scheduling processes, so it should be handled with care.

entry.title: Application Consultant SAP PP - Hybrid (Heidenheim, Germany)
entry.content:
HARTMANN GROUP, seeking a highly skilled Application Consultant SAP PP to join our dynamic IT team. In this role, you will be responsible for analyzing requirements, designing, configuring, and operating IT solutions based on standard processes. Your expertise will ensure the availability and scalability of our applications, contributing to the ongoing modernization and optimization of our IT landscape.

 Responsibilities:

  • Analyze requirements, design, configure, and operate IT solutions according to IT standard processes.
  • Understand, analyze, and document requirements; recommend appropriate solutions to ensure application availability and scalability.
  • Operate relevant IT applications and solutions in line with agreed service levels.
  • Select suitable hardware, software, and service providers to meet business needs.
  • Define architecture and platform or software basis for implementing and provisioning IT solutions.
  • Drive standardization, harmonization, and modernization of applications.
  • Identify process optimization and innovation potentials using IT tools and digitalization capabilities.
  • Take ownership of service delivery for respective solutions and services.

Qualifications:

  • University degree in IT or equivalent field.
  • Several years of practical experience with global applications in the assigned area.
  • Training/certification in one or more IT delivery processes (e.g., SCRUM, PMP, Prince, ITIL) is a plus.
  • Advanced skills in IT Requirements Engineering and Design Thinking, with a basic understanding of IT architectures.
  • Strong stakeholder management skills with a proven ability to build relationships with key contacts.
  • Experience in managing service providers and partners to deliver applications aligned with business objectives.
  • Proficiency in English and German.

This is a full time (hybrid) opportunity, kindly apply to the link or reach out to me directly. you can also connect with me on LinkedIn.

https://careers.hartmann.info/job-invite/6047/?utm_source=SAPCommunity

entry.title: Re: Can I dynamically switch between Screen Personas based on actions taken on-screen?
entry.content:
Of course. You can switch the flavor via scripting, based on input value.
session.utils.changeFlavor

Another way could be using different viewports and switching between those instead of another flavor. Then, you would need to deal with just a single flavor during flavor assignment which will make administration easier.

It really depends on the actual scenario. If the two layouts are very different across multiple screens, then having separate flavors is probably simpler. If the difference is only in one screen, then going the viewport way would be beneficial.

My extensive experience in HR, combined with the technical expertise gained through the DSI, will equip me to effectively implement technological solutions. This program will not only solidify my technical knowledge but also empower me to drive impactful changes within organizations by leveraging the strategic potential of SAP SuccessFactors and well-informed decision-making processes.

 

Beyond personal growth, I am particularly enthusiastic about the DSI's potential to position me as a role model and advocate for diversity within the tech industry. I am eager to contribute my unique perspective while actively acquiring new knowledge.

 

Thank you for your time and consideration. I am confident that my skills and aspirations align perfectly with the DSI program, and I look forward to the opportunity to discuss my qualifications 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Problem to change or assign payee of supplier documen...
entry.content:
Hey Guti,

This particular functionality is similar in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and On Premise.

I was getting the same result you shared in your screenshots (thank you for adding them since it makes a lot easier to understand the question!) and I was able to “see/enable” the “Payee” field.

The visibility of the "Payer" field in this particular app is related to the setup of the Supplier Master data. Specifically, you need to have Alternative Payees configured for the supplier in question. This enables the app to display additional fields that are pertinent to the payment transaction details.

Here's how you can solve this:

1. Navigate to the Supplier Master data screen.

2. Enter the relevant details for Alternative Payees within the supplier’s profile. Once you have added or confirmed these details, revisit the "Change Document" App. You should now see that the "Payer" field becomes visible and editable, allowing you to proceed with your tasks.

I've included a few screenshots from a test I performed in a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. These images should guide you through the process step-by-step. Please take your time to review them and replicate the steps in your system.

I hope this helps.

Ernesto Cepeda

entry.title: SAP COMMERCE CLOUD AND PROMOTION RECOMMENDATIONS WITH OPPS
entry.content:
SAP COMMERCE CLOUD AND PROMOTION RECOMMENDATIONS WITH OPPS

A brief about OPPS

SAP Omnichannel Promotion Pricing as a cloud-based service is a pricing and promotion solution for your sales channel applications , which ensures consistent pricing by running the same calculation logic based on the same price and promotional data.

OPP cloud service supports a wide range of digital sales channels, including e-commerce websites, third-party webshops, mobile apps, In-Store App (Physical store -POS) and social media shopping platforms. This diversity ensures that businesses can implement a cohesive promotion strategy across all customer touchpoints.

SAP provides standard integration with SAP Commerce Cloud (starting from version 1905.08). With a cloud-based solution, SAP handles updates and maintenance, ensuring that the system is always running on the latest version with the newest features and security patches.

Some useful links for OPPS:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-omnichannel-promotion-pricing-new-cloud-service/ba-p/13403710
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_INTEGRATIONS/631dbd4d570d41178895fd3ad14d87b1/7aae780bd0b84c86bb8038e65924cd97.html
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_INTEGRATIONS/631dbd4d570d41178895fd3ad14d87b1/19d0a3ea46fa46cf93276b12d60e3269.html
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_INTEGRATIONS/631dbd4d570d41178895fd3ad14d87b1/9281198f04594360b4c1efe34bb21afd.html

OPPS Promotion Recommendation feature

OPPS Promotion Recommendations in SAP Commerce Cloud, particularly when integrated with the Omnichannel Promotion Pricing Service (OPPS), serve a crucial role in enhancing the customer shopping experience by providing visibility into potential promotions during the purchasing journey.

With promotion recommendations, you can suggest customers to make changes to their shopping carts so that further promotions are granted.

Here’s how you can utilize Promotion Recommendations effectively in this context:

Use Case: Providing Information on Potential Promotions

1. Integration with OPPS:

Typically, the integration involves using the sappspricing extension in SAP Commerce Cloud. This integration allows the commerce system to call the OPPS calculation service to determine applied promotions based on eligibility criteria.

2. Current Limitations:

The standard integration primarily focuses on retrieving information about promotions that have already been applied, once eligibility conditions are met. This means that promotions available at the product level (visible on Product Detail Pages - PDP) are known, but basket-level promotions (such as bonus buys or conditional discounts) are not visible until they are applied in the cart.

3. Enhancing Customer Experience:

To address the scenario where customers seek information on potential promotions before adding items to the cart or during the browsing process, Promotion Recommendations within OPPS become valuable.

4. Implementation Considerations:

    • Configuration: Configure the OPPS to include Promotion Recommendations as part of the service.
    • Customization: Tailor the display of these recommendations to match the UI/UX of your commerce site.
    • API Integration: Ensure seamless integration between SAP Commerce Cloud and OPPS APIs to fetch and display real-time recommendations.

5. Benefits:

    • Improved Sales: By making potential promotions visible earlier in the customer journey, you can influence purchase decisions positively.
    • Enhanced Customer Engagement: Providing relevant promotions at the right time enhances the overall shopping experience.
    • Competitive Advantage: Differentiate your offering by providing transparent and attractive promotion details upfront.

6. Examples of Promotion Recommendations:

    • Display on Product Pages: By integrating Promotion Recommendations, you can display potential promotions on Product Detail Pages (PDP) based on the customer's current session context and eligibility.
    • Basket Level Promotions: This includes scenarios like "Buy A and get 10% discount on B", which can now be surfaced to the customer proactively, influencing their purchase decisions.
    • There can be other touchpoints as well across the website, where customer would like to upsell their promotions.

Below is an example , where we implemented potential promotions on PDP for a retail customer based out of Saudi Arabia , using promotion recommendations feature of OPPS.

 

To effectively implement Promotion Recommendations in SAP Commerce Cloud with OPPS (Omnichannel Promotion Pricing Service), and provide customers with information about potential promotions during their purchase journey, follow these steps and considerations:

Configuration in OPPS

  1. Mark Promotions as Available for Promotion Recommendations:
    • Ensure promotions are flagged as "Available for Promotion Recommendations" in OPPS Promo UI. This is set by configuring the promotion with the appropriate settings in your SAP Commerce setup.

 

    • Verify that your OPPS client API version is at least 9.0 to utilize this feature effectively.
    • In case you are using SAP CAR for maintaining promotions, make sure the CAR version is above 5.0 to use this feature.
      • Below attributes in  promotion rule should be set, in order to make it available for promotion recommendation
        • PROMO_REC_THRES_PERC
        • PROMO_REC_FLAG
    • For detailed steps on marking promotions and configurations, refer to the SAP documentation.

Implementation in SAP Commerce Cloud

1. Extend DefaultPPSRequestCreator:

    • Modify OPPS request to include Promotion Recommendations as part of the response.
    • Extend the standard OPPS request creator (DefaultPPSRequestCreator) in SAP Commerce Cloud to include the RecommendationMode parameter.
    • Set the OPPS version to 9.0 in your request to ensure compatibility with the promotion recommendation feature.

2. Extend OPPS Models:

    • As Promotion Recommendation models are not included in the out-of-the-box sapppspricing extension, extend your custom sappspricing extension to include necessary data models:
      • PromotionRecommendationBenefitsType
      • PromotionRecommendationMissingTriggersType
      • PromotionRecommendations
      • PromotionRecommendationsType
      • PromotionRecommendationTriggerBase
      • RecommendationModeTypeCodeEnumeration
      • RecommendationStatusTypeCodeEnumeration

Details on the structure of above data models can be found in the SAP API Hub.

3. Parse and Utilize Promotion Recommendations in SAP Commerce:

    • Once the OPPS response includes promotion recommendations, parse this data within SAP Commerce Cloud.
    • Implement logic to display these recommendations on your storefront, such as:
      • Displaying bonus buys on Product Detail Pages (PDP). Bundle request can be made to OPPS to get the price for  bonus buy bundle.
      • Showing potential promotions in the cart before checkout, similar to how SAP Commerce Cloud's native promotion engine functions.

4. Resources for Implementation:

    • Refer to sample requests and responses provided in the SAP documentation for guidance on formatting requests and handling responses.
    • Use these resources to integrate and customize the display of promotion recommendations according to your specific business requirements.

Conclusion

Incorporating Promotion Recommendations into your SAP Commerce Cloud setup with OPPS allows you to bridge the gap between backend promotions and frontend customer experience. It empowers customers with valuable information on potential promotions, thereby increasing engagement and driving sales. This proactive approach aligns well with modern retail strategies focused on customer-centric experiences and maximizing revenue opportunities.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.14
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.14 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.409 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Data Exploration
    • Live SAP BW - Report-Report Interface (RRI) | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Unit positions for SAP HANA based models
    • Data Analyzer Chart Enhancements
    • Support SAP Datasphere folders in Select Model dialog
    • Calculations Dialog: Conversion Functions
    • Calculation Editor Enhancements
  • Data Modeling
    • Standard Unit Conversion | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Enterprise Planning
    • Data actions and multi actions - support for account or measure input control parameters | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Plan Entry – data file upload (end-user) | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Planning Process Management – administrator option to group calendar notifications | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Value Driver Tree - styling flexibility for cascading style sheets | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Support Unit Conversion consumption in Commenting
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • Add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
      • Integration with Microsoft PowerPoint | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Mobile Analytics
    • Biometric support in Android mobile | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Story Design
    • New support in lite viewer | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Table: support of advanced filter with unbooked mode | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Table:
    • Descriptions for story calculations | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Vertical filter panel – reordering of page filters | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Story Extensibility
    • Cross-page pop-up widget | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Save story with scripts | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Section export printing
    • Composite Enhancement: Expose Widget Property as Composite Property Sheet
  • User Collaboration
    • Show last comment in comment widget | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Story comment configuration | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Schedule optimized story experience in PowerPoint format | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Schedule and deliver story content within the email body

 

Data Exploration

 

Live SAP BW - Report-Report Interface (RRI)

As a user of data analyzer you can now work with the report-to-report interface (RRI) in SAP BW live connections to jump from the executed query (sender) to another report (receiver) as maintained in the RRI back-end transaction (RSBBS). The following report types are supported: SAP BW Query, Transaction, Web Address, ABABP Report. The report-to-report interface opens the receiver report of type SAP BW Query in data analyzer. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

Note: Please consider the SAP BW version dependencies of SAP Analytics Cloud features when using the live connection. For more details on feature support, see SAP Note 2715030.

 

Unit positions for SAP HANA based models       

When working with SAP HANA based models in data analyzer, you can display the unit, currency, and scale for measures/accounts in the row, header, or value cell. As a data analyzer user, you can use this selection and store this setting in an insight.

Note: Please consider the SAP HANA EPM MDS version dependency using the live connection, as outlined in SAP Note 2444261.

 

Data Analyzer Chart Enhancements

For charts in data analyzer several user experience enhancements have been made and a few known issues have been addressed.

  • Improved responsiveness of charts:
  • moved data labels out of the chart area
  • adjusted scroll bars

 

Support SAP Datasphere folders in Select Model dialog

When opening an SAP Datasphere connection in the Select Model dialog in data analyzer for starting a new analysis, you can now sort the SAP Datasphere spaces by name, description, and owner.

In addition, in the Select Model dialog, you can now see folders within the spaces and navigate them.

 

Calculations Dialog: Conversion Functions

As a user of the calculation editor in data analyzer you can now use the following conversion functions when creating formulas:

  • DECFLOAT
  • DOUBLE
  • FLOAT
  • INT
  • FLOOR
 
Calculation Editor Enhancements

The calculation editor has been enhanced through tokenization, to improve clarity and user-friendliness. Each object  dimensions, dimension properties, dimension hierarchies or measures) can now be removed as a token.

 

Data Modeling

 

Standard Unit Conversion

It is now possible to create a conversion measure of type Unit on models that contain measures. A unit conversion measure can convert source values from the base measure to the specified target units, that belong to the same unit group, using values from the Standard Unit Conversion table.

You can also create additional unit conversion measures, add calculated measures based on the conversion, or work with the converted values in your stories and analytics applications. The Standard Unit Conversion table is available for reference from the Conversions tab of the Modeler. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

To learn more, check out this blog post by @AmandaJMurphy.

Enterprise Planning

 

Data actions and multi actions - support for account or measure input control parameters

When setting up starters for data actions or multi actions, you can now use measures and accounts as input controls. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Plan Entry – data file upload (end-user)  

Users can now directly upload data from a local Excel or CSV file. An upload job must be created in the data management workspace and then referenced in a Starter widget within the story. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Planning Process Management – administrator option to group calendar notifications

Administrators can now group the calendar notification emails into a summary email and send them daily at a specific time to all users. Note that reminder emails and summary emails from bulk editing are excluded from this grouping and are sent separately. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Value Driver Tree - styling flexibility for cascading style sheets

Uses can now change all colors of values drive tree widgets with story theming and cascading style sheets (CSS). (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Support Unit Conversion consumption in Commenting

In tables, you can now add data point comments to data cells sourced from unit conversion measures. In comments, the unit will also be displayed for Original Value of data cells.

 

Microsoft Office Integration

 

Add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint

 

Integration with Microsoft PowerPoint

This is the initial release of the SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint. The add-in is a fully web-based offering enabling you to embed SAP Analytics Cloud widgets in your PowerPoint presentations. You can refresh the widget's data at any time to bring accurate and trusted data into your presentations without the need to copy/paste the information from your SAP Analytics Cloud system again. For more information about the add-in, please check the SAP Help Portal at SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Mobile Analytics

 

Biometric support in Android mobile  

Users can now use TouchID to login to the SAP Analytics Cloud Android mobile app. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Story Design

 

New support in lite viewer  

The lite viewer now supports: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • Choropleth layer in geo maps
  • Stories in view mode.
  • Stories in presentation mode with:
    • the presentation structure configured
    • a collapsed navigation panel
    • a hidden filter or navigation button in the toolbar

 

Table: support of advanced filter with unbooked mode

In the optimized story experience, you can now hide unbooked members that are not needed when you create table layouts with advanced filters, which is especially important for ad-hoc planning. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Descriptions for story calculations

Calculation descriptions can now contain dynamic text, which will update the name of the calculation displayed in story widgets, based on the selected input values. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Vertical filter panel – reordering of page filters

In the optimized story experience, story designers can now change the order of page filters in the filter panel settings, in the same way that story filters can be ordered. The defined order is applied to the vertical filter panel which only has a page filters section, in view and edit mode, and will be saved with the story. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Story Extensibility

 

Cross-page pop-up widget

As a story developer in the optimized story experience, you can now create a story popup, which can be used by any page in the story. You can also convert an existing page popup to story popup. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Save story with scripts

Story developers can now select Enable designers to edit and save story with scripts in Story Details, so that story designers can edit and save the story with scripts instead of having a read-only view. The scripts remain hidden to them, and they are warned when deleting items that contain or are referenced in scripts. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Section export printing

Section widgets are now supported for PDF or PPTX export in the optimized story experience. As a story developer, you can include sections when configuring the Export to PDF or Export to PPTX technical object so that they can be exported via the script APIs as well.

 

Composite Enhancement: Expose Widget Property as Composite Property Sheet

As a composite creator, you can define an interface property to let story designers customize it based on the story context. You can define either a Boolean property for the view time visibility of specific widgets, or a String property for text in specific widget elements in your composite.

As a story designer in the optimized story experience, you can therefore modify the property defined and exposed by the composite creator, such as a chart title, table’s hyperlink URL, or button’s view time visibility within the composite in your story.

 

User Collaboration

 

Show last comment in comment widget 

In stories, on the comment widget you can now choose to view the last added comment. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Story comment configuration

A toggle is now available to enable or disable comments within a story. By disabling story comments, users will not be able to add or view comments. However, they can still work on the datapoint and dimension comments. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Schedule optimized story experience in PowerPoint format  

Story publications can now be scheduled in PowerPoint (.pptx) format. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Schedule and deliver story content within the email body

Using Email Delivery Options, you can now choose among the following options to publish your story:

  • Attachment
  • Preview Inline
  • Attachment and Preview Inline 

If you select Preview Inline the Story content would be delivered in the email body.

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Unleashing Centralized Digital Experience for Suppliers – SAP Build Workzone
entry.content:
A Wave of Modern Digital Workplace

In today's business landscape, digitalization and innovation pose significant challenges for companies. To meet the ever-evolving needs of customers, businesses must swiftly adopt cloud solutions. However, progress is often hindered by limited resources and the complexities of maintaining IT infrastructures with multiple applications. To overcome these challenges, companies need to adopt digital processes, reduce technological obstacles, and empower both experts and citizen developers to drive innovation. 

This blog post will explore how SAP Build Workzone empowers companies to unleash the full potential of their central digital workplace, creating a more connected and efficient ecosystem for suppliers.


Background & Business Challenges

Customer is committed to becoming an Intelligent Enterprise by leveraging the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). To achieve this, they are dedicated to modernizing and replacing outdated business processes, empowering IT professionals and business users with tools to efficiently create engaging business sites for employees, partners, suppliers, and customers. A suitable replacement for the current landscape of supplier resources and applications is imperative. The scattered information throughout the enterprise lacks a centralized access point, causing inefficiencies in navigating through numerous disconnected systems. Decentralized content creation further hinders the development of flexible business sites and pages. The customer’s objective is to boost user productivity and engagement through interactive workspaces, forums, and knowledge-based articles. By centralizing information and facilitating efficient content creation, the company aims to create a seamless and productive environment for all users.

 

Customer's Vision to Elevate Supplier Engagement

The vision for the Supplier Portal is the ultimate centralized platform for Vendors/Suppliers and external partners. The aim is to provide easy access to all necessary resources in one location, including the Supplier Handbook, Supplier Resources, Proxied Applications, Contingent Worker Resources, and the option to access the Restricted Supplier Portal content. The customer worked with the SAP MaxAttention team to create a Digital Workplace that is agile and consistent with SAP's user experience. In this process, the team incorporated the latest innovations of BTP to provide a better user experience and easier content management. After a successful proof-of-concept, the team confidently deployed the updated content for productive usage through internal and external user onboarding processes

 

SAP MaxAttention Engagement

The success of this project is a true testament to SAP MaxAttention’s expertise and commitment to delivering exceptional results. During this engagement, SAP MaxAttention team worked along with SAP Build Work Zone Product Development and the customer’s CoE teams. This collaboration resulted in quick ROI and increased value by maximizing existing SAP solutions, specifically in the space of SAP Build subscription. SAP Build is powered by SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) which enables business experts to easily create and augment enterprise applications, automate tedious processes, and design engaging business sites by using visual tools and a rich set of services and pre-built content. SAP Build capabilities include:

  • SAP Build Code – SAP Build Code provides generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, optimized for Java and JavaScript application development. It offers a turn-key environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management.
  • SAP Build Apps – Evolution of AppGyver to build mobile and web applications with a pixel-perfect design using the drag-and-drop functionality and a rich component library.
  • SAP Build Process Automation – Offers task and process automation leveraging robotic process automation (RPA) bots, workflows, forms, embedded artificial intelligence (AI), business rules, and decisions.
  • SAP Build Work Zone – Allows the creation of enterprise-grade custom business sites using new capabilities such as a flexible page builder and context-aware integrated UI cards.

Architecture

Below is the architecture of the digital Supplier Portal in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.

Delivery Scope

Setup of SAP Build Work Zone

For the platform preparation, we started with onboarding activities in SAP BTP, for example, Sub-account setup, setting up SAML Trust with PingIDP for authentication, user management & provisioning, and authorization management, data privacy, workspace setup to configure areas and workspace.

Building a Central Entry Point (Home Page)

The company's home page has been redesigned and now serves as the landing page for the website, featuring protected content. The customer team has been given the authority to distinguish between the administrative aspects of the SAP Build Work Zone landing page and to use pre-built templates and layouts to implement best practices. The objective is to differentiate between the usage of different widgets and to decide whether to build a Workspace or leave the content on the Home Page.

Supplier Handbook

Supplier Handbook workspace hosts a collection of key resources and assets (e.g., revision-controlled documents) used by customer’s Vendors/Suppliers. Private workspace access is only for onboard suppliers. Some of the key requirements were to have an intuitive user experience, manage contents and their versions, and send communication to the stakeholders whenever changes are made to the contents. 

Supplier Resources

Supplier Resources is a public workspace that hosts a collection of resources and assets needed for supplier onboarding. The customer’s prospective and established suppliers can find all the publicly available documents, process documentation, and answers to frequently asked questions related to the customer’s Account Payable, Invoicing Process, etc. This workspace also provides an entry point to the Supplier Handbook that provides access to protected resources.

Just-In-Time Reports Integration via SAP Build Process Automation

One of the requirements of the project is to enable vendors to view stocking reports in real-time. To achieve this, we leveraged SAP Build Process Automation.  SAP Build Process Automation is a citizen developer solution to adapt, improve, and innovate business processes with no-code workflow management and robotic process automation capabilities.
Using this tool, we developed unattended BOT to publish reports from BI and S/4HANA. On a periodical basis, automatic synch between the FTP and the SAP Build Work Zone is triggered. This business requirement was prototyped using SAP Build Work Zone OData API. The below diagrams illustrate the technical architecture of the solution.  

Supplier Registration Forms

To fulfill the need for supplier diversity, the existing registration form for suppliers to request access to a private workspace contains various form controls. The form includes multipage reports, and dynamic content, and retains automatic answers to meet the key requirements.

Outcomes

Empowered Business Users with Enterprise-Grade Business Sites

SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition enables business users to boost productivity and engagement by creating digital workplace solutions. This platform provides a central entry point for accessing business processes, applications, communication, and information from any device.

Business Value:

  • Improve productivity by accessing apps, processes, and information all in one place.
  • Experience a seamlessly integrated environment across SAP and third-party apps to accommodate collaboration tools and integrated information
  • With SAP Build Work Zone as a collaboration platform, users will have all the resources needed to make informed decisions faster
  • Say goodbye to clunky workflows, and hello to a world of efficient business apps, reports, and information that promote collaboration and drive success

Key Milestones:

  • Project Duration: < 4 months
  • Unified and Intelligent work experience by providing central access to apps, tasks, notifications, information, and communication.              
  • Optimize Business Processes with employees, partners, customers & suppliers.
  • Reduce TCO by leveraging existing investments in the SAP Business Technology Platform and harmonization of existing systems and repositories.
  • Integrated collaboration capabilities that can be extended to other business processes
  • Seamless SAP and third-party apps integration
  • Establish Central Inbox

Following are some of the business KPIs:

Contact persons:

SAP MaxAttention CoE: Kiran Kola, Rohit Dwivedi 

SAP Product Management: Florian Buech

entry.title: Re: Search in SEN | book pages
entry.content:
Hi,

Searches in Manager will locate text on Book Pages.  This is also the case for content published as a library and accessed through Manager.  I suspect that this feature only works if your Manager environment is installed on HANA, not SQL Server, so if you are using the On Premise version of SAP Enable Now and your Manager is installed on SQL Server, it likely will not work.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Track changes in Physical Inventory Count
entry.content:
Dear riyamd,

You can track changes made to physical inventory counts using the Manage Physical Inventory Documents  APP. This app displays the physical inventory data for a physical inventory document and for a physical inventory count. For more information, see Manage Physical Inventory Documents.  Also there is the Process Physical Inventory Count Results APP, which generates a list of the inventory differences for one or more materials. This list can, for example, be used for a checklist after a physical inventory count. For more information, see Process Physical Inventory Count Results.

Please note that this app only displays changes that were made after the physical inventory document was created. It does not display changes made during the creation of the physical inventory document.

Best regards,

Geovana

entry.title: Re: Track changes in Physical Inventory Count
entry.content:
Dear riyamd,


You can track changes made to physical inventory counts using the Manage Physical Inventory Documents  APP. This app displays the physical inventory data for a physical inventory document and for a physical inventory count. For more information, see Manage Physical Inventory Documents.  Also there is the Process Physical Inventory Count Results APP, which generates a list of the inventory differences for one or more materials. This list can, for example, be used for a checklist after a physical inventory count. For more information, see Process Physical Inventory Count Results.

Please note that this app only displays changes that were made after the physical inventory document was created. It does not display changes made during the creation of the physical inventory document.

Best regards,

 

Geovana

entry.title: Re: Enable Now - Learner View - Project Tracking
entry.content:
Hi Andrew,

The issue is likely that the Simulation is not making it to the end macro; it must make it to the end macro to show as complete.

 If you Simulation is structured like the example in the image, you will need to add a button to the end page of the Simulation that lets the user proceed to the end macro.  Add a button that says something like Complete Simulation and add a link action to that button that take the Simulation to the next step so it makes it to the end macro.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: How can I create and Confirm Warehouse Task in one st...
entry.content:
Dear Amod_V,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can create and confirm a Warehouse Task (WT) in one step using the API ‘api_warehouse_order_task_2/srvd_a2x’. However, the ‘Confirm Immediately’ and ‘Propose Confirmation’ settings in the Warehouse Process Type (WPT) only apply when creating the WT in the foreground, not when using the API.

If you’re encountering errors when trying to confirm the WT using the ‘SAP__self.ConfirmWarehouseTaskExact’ call, it might be because the system is expecting a resource to be assigned. Even if you’re not using Warehouse Orders or RF, the system might still require a resource for confirmation. Unfortunately, there isn’t a configuration setting that allows you to bypass this requirement when using the API. You might need to assign a dummy resource or use a different method to achieve your requirement of creating and immediately confirming a WT via API.

If it's not this message you are receiving, if you could share the message here with us, then I can check if something else could be missing to help with this confirmation. 

Thank you and best regards,

Geovana

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now Learner Third party access
entry.content:
Hi David,

It is possible to grant access to externals to Enable Now content but all users, external or not, accessing live Enable Now content must be licensed.  Your choices are:

1. Generate PDF documentation for the external users.  Check your licensing agreement for Enable Now but I think this can be accessed without a license
2. Create manual user accounts for the external users in Enable Now and then have them log into those accounts using the URL https://customername.enable-now.cloud.sap/formlogin/ - you would obviously replace customername with the name piece of your Enable Now URL.  The external users could then access content from the Learner View tab, if it has been assigned to them.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: NF-e Technical Note 2024.001 1.10 Version: Recent Updates on Nota Fiscal Eletrônica
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil team has released updates on the Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e).

The update complies with SEFAZ NF-e Technical Note 2024.001 1.10 Version. The new version is planned to be in production on September 2, 2024, according to the tax authority.

NF-e Technical Note 2024.001 1.10 Version

The new updates on the electronic notas fiscais (model 55) replace the denial process with the rejection process and add the option 4 (Simples Nacional - Microempreendedor Individual - MEI) in the Tax Regimen Code (Código de Regime Tributário) field.

You can implement these changes in the outbound invoicing option for Brazil by following the instructions on the SAP Note 3487670 and the SAP Note 3488179.

If you use inbound invoicing option for Brazil, follow the instructions on the SAP Note 3488179.

You can also implement the above SAP Notes in the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP NFE) solution.

Related blog posts:

  • Usage Analytics Dashboard
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Inbound Nota Fiscal

For a step-by-step guide on how to use the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing option for Brazil, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound invoicing option for Brazil

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN
  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content.

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

Tags: brazil, brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal, outbound, nf-e, grc, technical note, 2024.001, crt, nt 2024.001, j_1bcrt, changes for individual microentrepreneur, documento fiscal eletronico, electronic fiscal document, simples nacional, microempreendedor individual, mei, micro empreendedor individual, código de regime tributário, crt, denial, rejection, status code, denied, denegação

entry.title: Re: Blocking the release of production order
entry.content:
Hello, 

You are on the right track with with what you have done already in opjk and setting to block release if Missing parts. 

In addition, you can try setting the availability check control for the ATP check for the components for the production order rule to do ATP at storage location level as opposed to plant level. 

You can do this in OPJJ in no storage location inspection and make sure that is blank/not checked. 

The check will only be made against components sitting in FU01 location. 

Make sure component is using such atp check in mrp3

Make sure that the issue storage location in the production order is set to FU01. 

You can drive that from the material master in Storage location field in MRP 2, can also do it from the master bom and set the Sloc in bom items for the components and can also do it manually on the production order before running MRP. 

Many ways to drive it for sure. 

What is going to happen now is that upon running the ATP check against the production order, it will check for availability in the FU01 location only.

If there is no sufficient stock in that storage location, because of the config you did for the order control (opjk) to prevent release if missing parts, then it will not allow release and you get what you need.

 

Please let me know if this helps. 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Disable "register" button SAP Cloud identity services
entry.content:
Please use the setting in the following link to configure the application to restrict access to specific users only:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-user-access-to-application

You have set to "Public" so you can see Register button on logon page. If you would like allow existing users only to logon, please change the setting to "Internal".

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Cycle Count IM
entry.content:
Hello Ashali,

When you create the job for Cycle Counting, may I suggest you to set some parameters like: 

  • Storage Location
  • Material Number
  • Material Type
  • Material Group
  • Stock Types

Parameters like those, can help you to narrow your physical inventory according to your physical inventory planning.

Hope this helps, regards,

Gabriel

entry.title: Re: How to get MTBF, MTTR reports in SAP Public cloud ?
entry.content:
Google “Fiori apps reference library”, use the filters to get to public cloud and then search for “Technical Object Damages” and “Technical Object Breakdowns” - those are the two standard Fiori apps.

also on that library, when you clicked on the relevant app, there is a section that talks to any installation requirements, or dependent configuration required.

Tim

entry.title: A Table Can Be Joined With a Maximum of one other table using Left Outer Join Error
entry.content:
hi, trying to migrate PP work center data. so got a query which works fine on ABAP 7.4 but it doesn't work on Old ERP Client which uses ABAP 7.3

the query is shown as below

  SELECT crhd~werks
         crhd~objty
         crhd~objid
        crhh~name AS hname
        crhd_up~arbpl AS arbpl_up
        crhd~arbpl
        crhd~verwe
        crtx~ktext AS ktext1
        crhd~veran
        crhd~planv
        crhd~rgekz
        crhd~vgwts
        crhd~steus
        crhd~vge01
        crhd~vge02
        crhd~vge03
        crhd~vge04
        crhd~vge05
        crhd~vge06
        kako~kapar AS kapar1
        crca~fork1 AS fork11
        crca~fork2 AS fork12
        crca~fork3 AS fork13
        crca~forkn AS forkn1
        crca~vert1
        crca~vertn
        kako~kapid
        kakt~ktext AS ktext2
        kako~planr
        kako~mosid
        kako~kalid
        kako~versa
        kako~meins
        kako~begzt
        kako~endzt
        kako~pause
        kako~ngrad
        kako~aznor
        kako~kapeh
        kako~kapter
        kako~kapavo
        kako~kaplpl
        kazy~anzhl
*        kazy~datuv,
        kazy~datub
        kazy~sprog
        kazy~anztg
        kazy~anzsh
        kazy~fabtg
        kako2~kapar AS kapar2
        crhd~fort1
        crhd~fort2
        crhd~fort3
        crhd~fortn
   FROM crhd
  LEFT JOIN crhs ON crhd~objty = crhs~objty_ho
                AND crhd~objid = crhs~objid_ho
  LEFT JOIN crhh ON crhs~objty_hy = crhh~objty
                AND crhs~objid_hy = crhh~objid
  LEFT JOIN crhd AS crhd_up ON crhs~objty_up = crhd_up~objty
                           AND crhs~objid_up = crhd_up~objid
  LEFT JOIN crca ON crhd~objty = crca~objty
                AND crhd~objid = crca~objid
  LEFT JOIN crtx ON crhd~objty = crtx~objty
                AND crhd~objid = crtx~objid
                AND crtx~spras = sy-langu
  LEFT JOIN kako ON crca~kapid = kako~kapid
  LEFT JOIN kakt ON kako~kapid = kakt~kapid
                AND kakt~spras = sy-langu
  LEFT JOIN kazy ON kako~kapid = kazy~kapid
                AND kazy~kkopf = abap_false
  LEFT JOIN kako AS kako2 ON crhd~kapid = kako2~kapid
 WHERE crhd~werks IN s_werks
   AND crhd~arbpl IN s_arbpl
   AND crhd~verwe IN s_verwe
   AND crhd~veran IN s_veran
   AND crhd~lvorm IN lt_lvorm
  ORDER BY crhd~werks crhd~arbpl crhd~verwe
 INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE lt_workcent.

when running those queries on ABAP 7.3 it occurs below errors

 - Unable to compare with "CRHS~OBJTY_HY". A tale can be joined with a maximum of one other table using LEFT OUTER JOIN. JOIN.

 

seems like LEFT JOIN didnt work as i expected on ABAP 7.3 or i should switch the LEFT JOIN sequences. it would be grateful if you guys give me some advice.

entry.title: Новый реферальный код Bybit: "BET1000" - обновление преимуществ для новых пользователей
entry.content: Хотите узнать о реферальном коде Bybit? С 16 июля новые пользователи смогут воспользоваться эксклюзивным кодом «BET1000» при регистрации учетной записи в 2024 году. Чтобы начать, посетите веб-сайт или мобильное приложение Bybit, нажмите «Зарегистрироваться» и введите свои данные вместе с реферальным кодом «BET1000». "или используйте номер своего мобильного телефона. Согласитесь с условиями, подтвердите свой адрес электронной почты или мобильный телефон и нажмите «Зарегистрироваться». Используя пригласительный код Bybit «BET1000», новые трейдеры получат существенный стартовый бонус в размере 1000 долларов США. Этот бонусный пакет включает в себя загадочную коробку стоимостью 500 долларов США и скидку 30% на торговые комиссии, что дает возможность заработать до 30 000 долларов США в качестве максимального бонуса за регистрацию. entry.title: Re: How can we add the Private Key Alias in the channel c...
entry.content:
Hello,

Certainly! It seems you’re encountering an issue with the Private Key Alias in the TPM configuration for the Trading Partner AS2 Sender channel. Let’s troubleshoot this:

Private Key Alias for Decryption (Inbound):
The Company (typically the B2B system owner) owns a public-private key pair.
The public key is shared with partners, who encrypt sensitive content using it.
To decrypt messages, the Company uses its private key.
Configure the private key alias in the Security tab of the Company Profile against the user who sends messages to integration flows at runtime.
Signature Verification (Inbound for both messages & MDN):
The Trading partner (your partner) owns a key pair.
They share their public key/certificate with your Company.
When sending messages, the partner creates a message digest and signature using their private key.
Your Company verifies the signature using the partner’s public key.
Upload the partner’s public certificate to the Certificate tab of the Trading Partner profile, and configure signature verification in the Security tab against the AS2 Partner ID.
Encryption (Outbound):
Your Company encrypts data using the partner’s public key.
On the partner’s side, the private key is used to decrypt the message.
Configure AS2 Receiver Communication under Systems tab of the Company/Trading Partner Profiles.
Signing (Outbound for both messages & MDN):  florida blue member login
Your Company creates a message digest and signature using its own private key.
The partner validates the signature using your Company’s public key.
Configure AS2 Receiver Communication under Systems tab of the Company/Trading Partner Profiles.

Best Regards

esther598

entry.title: Enablement Configuration Setting for Stories in People Analytics
entry.content:
Have you been wondering how to ensure all the enablement configurations are set up in SAP SuccessFactors to ensure Stories in People Analytics are working? Look no further than this IDP - SAP SuccessFactors Stories in People Analytics: Enablement Configurations - and this blog introducing this topic to you.

Let us examine the initial and post-activation enablement configuration settings to ensure Story reports work seamlessly in your SAP SuccessFactors system. For IPS connector details, please read through the Section 5 of the IDP before executing Step 1. If you are still using SF ODATA IPS connector, we recommend you to migrate from ODATA to SCIM IPS connector before initiating the Stories in People Analytics Upgrade Center task.

There are 4 Steps to follow before you can start using Stories in People Analytics, as mentioned below:

Step 1: Enable Stories via Upgrade Center Task

Log in to SuccessFactors > Go to Admin Center> Navigate to Upgrade Center. Choose 'Stories in People Analytics' and upgrade. Check for a successful upgrade via the “View Recently Completed Upgrades” link in the Upgrade Center. Once the task is successfully submitted, you may have to wait for 45-60 minutes for backend configuration jobs to successfully complete. 

                                                         Upgrade Center

Post- Enablement Steps

Step 2: Conditional Authentication configuration for Corporate Identity provider

If your organization does not use Corporate Identity Provider, you can skip to Step 3: IPS user sync. If your organization has Corporate Identity Provider, please follow the below guidelines to add additional settings, validate and ensure configuration in Identity Authentication is compatible to run Story reports.

a) Log on to the Identity Authentication Service as an IAS admin and go to Applications & Resources, select the Applications tab.

b) In the Bundled Applications section, choose the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant (mostly configured as SF Analytics). It is configured either at the same level as the SAP SuccessFactors tenant, or under the SAP SuccessFactors tenant as its child application.

c) Edit the Conditional Authentication details.

d) Ensuring that the set of rules used for the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant and the SAP SuccessFactors tenant are the same. This should be already set in SAP analytics cloud tenant is tagged as a child application to SuccessFactors tenant.

e) In the Default Identity Provider field, select the Identity Provider that you are using to authenticate users on the SAP SuccessFactors tenant, and save the changes.

Go to Identity Providers, select the Corporate Identity Providers tab, and select your corporate identity provider. 

  • Ensure that the Forward All SSO Requests to Corporate IdP option in the SINGLE SIGNON section has been disabled.
  • Configure Identity Federation, enable the Use Identity Authentication user store option in the User Store section, and save the changes.

Before activating Stories, if you need help with setting up Corporate IdP with IAS / SuccessFactors, please review detailed steps and SF-IAS architecture in the KBA - Configuring SSO between Corporate IdP, Identity Authentication tenant and SuccessFactors.

Step 3: IPS user sync

At this step, it is important to know if your systems are configured with ODATA IPS connector or SCIM IPS connector to synchronize users between SAP SuccessFactors, IAS, and SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.  If you are Using ODATA IPS connector, you will need to ensure below permissions. • Manage Integration Tool- Allow Admin to Access OData API through Basic Authentication • Manage User Employee Export Using SCIM IPS connector? SCIM IPS connector with client certificate authentication, does not have any pre-requisites of permissions.

For a more detailed description of step 3 along with system screenshots, I invite you to read through page 12 of the IDP.

Step 4: Grant Story report permissions

We strongly recommend following the help guide documentation to set up permission related to Story Reports. Some modules (ex. RCM/PM/GM etc.) require additional permissions to ensure a user can report on schema specific data. Link to help guide - Set up Permission for Story report.

There are other topics of interest like the FAQs, important notes on if your systems are configured on SCIM IPS connector and you want to ensure permission-based sync relevant configurations and attribute, performing user synchronization that are mentioned in the IDP.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflo...
entry.content:
I do not know if there is a special shortcut in SAP Business Application Studio as there is in SAP Build Apps, but you probably do this best by calling the SAP Build Process Automation APIS:

https://api.sap.com/package/SAPProcessAutomation/rest

The triggering API is under the Workflow tile. You need a service key and destination (generally created for you when you install Process Automation).

entry.title: Re: SAP Build app transaction data not being saved to Visual cloud function DB
entry.content: I am assuming your data variable is of type new (not list or single record) - this is an important piece of information. And as @Jakub_Roguski mentioned, best to show where you populate that "new" type of data variable, which is empty by default. Also, it is worthwhile checking the Developer Tools when clicking the submit button, because you can see the payload being sent and therefore whether the record is being populated.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build app transaction data not being saved to Visual cloud function DB
entry.content: I am assuming your data variable is of type new (not list or single record) - this is an important piece of information.

entry.title: Re: eInvoice Email-Process - Process Status is stuck on "Request Acknowledged" in the eDoc
entry.content:
Dear Lukas,

thank you for reaching out to us. The status in the backend system shall update, to allow you further actions like sending the mail. If you have walked through the steps listed here Integrating Peppol Exchange with SAP S/4HANA Cloud | SAP Help Portal, and there is no error in the document monitor, and the document does not update in the backend system, please open an incident for component LOD-LH-DRC-PAP, so we can have a look into it.

For step Setting Up Connectivity | SAP Help Portal, please check the following instructions:

  1. In the BTP Subaccount, go to Connectivity -> Destination and go to "Certificates"
    1. Choose "Generate New Certificate"
      1. Certificate Name - any meaningful name
        File Extension PFX
        Remember the Password
    2. Select the new certificate in your destination to the backend system and provide Authentication "Internet" and "ClientCertificateAuthentication"
      1. your Certificate needs to be selected in "Key Store Location"
      2. Provide the Password from above.
      3. Save the Destination
      4.  Export the private certificate as PFX file
    3. Verify the path of your Destination, according to Creating Communication Arrangements | SAP Help Portal , ...Inbound Communication > Inbound Services | The system automatically creates the service URL when you create the communication arrangement. You must enter this URL in the Destination in SAP BTP. See Setting Up Connectivity....
  2. Open the certificate locally and import to your PC's key store
    1. provide your password when asked
    2. go to your local PC's Key Store ("Manage User Certificates" in Windows)
    3. find your certificate and export  it to your local drive
      1. do not export the private key
  3. In S/4 HANA Cloud Switch to your Communication System with Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0529 (the current Communication system for Peppol Exchange)
    1. upload the certificate to your existing Communication User
  4. Reprocess your Documents in the Peppol Exchange App.
  5. If you still receive the error, the Participant might have to be deleted and recreated

Regards,

Uwe

entry.title: Start Time ,end time & paygrade mapping in document generation not working
entry.content:
Hi team,

I hope this message finds you well. I wanted to bring to your attention an issue we've encountered with our document generation process. Specifically, we've mapped the start time and end time of short leaves in our template, but unfortunately, this data is not reflecting in the custom mail notifications.

 

  1. start time & end time is not reflecting in mail notification
  2. pay grade is not reflecting in mail notification

    We only are able to map reference fields in the leave template & unable to find the fields in reference. If we are mapping start time, end time & paygrade of the employee as direct fields, in custom mail notification its not appearing. PFA the screenshot.

    Could you please look into this matter at your earliest convenience? It seems there may be a disconnect between how the data is mapped in the template and how it appears in the notifications.

    Your prompt attention to this issue would be greatly appreciated.

entry.content:
Greetings,

can you confirm that you have properly setup the activity in the action box:

  1. Function module: QMLR_CREATE_INSP_LOT
